text: To list directory contents in detailed long format (showing permissions, ownership, size, and modification date), you run the command {{c1::`ls -l`}}.
explanation: The `-l` flag changes the listing style to a detailed, column-oriented long format, which is essential for checking permissions, ownership, and metadata.
spanish: Para listar el contenido de un directorio en formato largo detallado (mostrando permisos, propietario, tamaño y fecha de modificación), ejecutas el comando ls -l.

text: To change the current working directory back to the user's home directory immediately, you use the command {{c1::`cd ~`}}.
explanation: In Linux shells, the tilde character `~` is a shortcut that represents the home directory of the currently logged-in user. Running `cd` with no arguments also defaults to this home directory.
spanish: Para cambiar el directorio de trabajo actual de regreso al directorio de inicio del usuario de inmediato, utilizas el comando cd ~.

text: To create a directory structure containing nested parent folders that do not exist yet, you use the command `mkdir` with the {{c1::`-p` flag}}.
explanation: The `-p` (parents) flag tells `mkdir` to create any missing intermediate directories in the path without throwing an error if the directory already exists.
spanish: Para crear una estructura de directorios que contiene carpetas padre anidadas que aún no existen, utilizas el comando mkdir con la opción -p.

text: In Linux permission management, the octal representation {{c1::`755`}} grants read, write, and execute permissions to the owner, and read and execute to group and others.
explanation: Octal permissions represent owner (7 = 4+2+1, rwx), group (5 = 4+1, r-x), and others (5 = 4+1, r-x). Writing it as rwxr-xr-x enables binary representation evaluation.
spanish: En la gestión de permisos de Linux, la representación octal 755 otorga permisos de lectura, escritura y ejecución al propietario, y de lectura y ejecución al grupo y a otros.

text: To change both the owner and the group of a file simultaneously, you use the command `chown` in the format {{c1::`owner:group`}}.
explanation: The colon `:` separates the target owner username and the target group name when executing chown. This allows single-command updates to file access restrictions.
spanish: Para cambiar tanto el propietario como el grupo de un archivo simultáneamente, utilizas el comando chown en el formato owner:group.

text: To redirect the standard error (stderr) of a command to a file, replacing its contents, you append {{c1::`2>`}} followed by the file path.
explanation: In Linux, file descriptor 1 is stdout, and file descriptor 2 is stderr. Using `2>` redirects only the error stream while letting stdout stream normally.
spanish: Para redirigir la salida de error estándar (stderr) de un comando a un archivo, reemplazando su contenido, agregas 2> seguido de la ruta del archivo.

text: To search for a text pattern in a file while ignoring case sensitivity, you use the `grep` command with the {{c1::`-i` flag}}.
explanation: The `-i` flag forces grep to perform a case-insensitive search, matching both uppercase and lowercase characters (e.g. matching 'Error', 'ERROR', and 'error').
spanish: Para buscar un patrón de texto en un archivo ignorando la distinción entre mayúsculas y minúsculas, utilizas el comando grep con la opción -i.

text: To replace all occurrences of a string within a file globally in-place using sed, you run `sed -i` with the substitution expression {{c1::`s/old/new/g`}}.
explanation: The `s` denotes substitute, `g` makes it global across each line (instead of just replacing the first occurrence on each line), and `-i` writes changes directly back to the file.
spanish: Para reemplazar todas las apariciones de una cadena dentro de un archivo globalmente en el lugar usando sed, ejecutas sed -i con la expresión de sustitución s/old/new/g.

text: To print the second column of a space-delimited text output, you feed it into awk using the action {{c1::`{print $2}`}}.
explanation: Awk automatically splits lines into fields using whitespace delimiters; `$0` represents the whole line, `$1` the first column, `$2` the second column, etc.
spanish: Para imprimir la segunda columna de una salida de texto delimitada por espacios, la pasas a awk usando la acción {print $2}.

text: To install a package in Debian-based systems without prompting the user for confirmation, you append the {{c1::`-y` flag}} to `apt-get install`.
explanation: The `-y` flag answers 'yes' automatically to all prompts during package installation, which is critical for writing unattended provisioning or CI/CD scripts.
spanish: Para instalar un paquete en sistemas basados en Debian sin solicitar confirmación al usuario, agregas la opción -y a apt-get install.

text: Custom systemd service unit files created by system administrators should be placed in the directory {{c1::`/etc/systemd/system/`}}.
explanation: The `/etc/systemd/system/` directory has precedence over vendor-supplied service definitions in `/lib/systemd/system/` and is reserved for administrator-defined services.
spanish: Los archivos de unidad de servicio systemd personalizados creados por los administradores de sistemas deben colocarse en el directorio /etc/systemd/system/.

scenario: Linux Engineering 🛠️: Real-Time Service Log Monitoring
text: To follow/tail syslog records in real-time using journalctl for a specific systemd unit, you use the command `journalctl` with the flags {{c1::`-u <unit> -f`}}.
explanation: The `-u` flag specifies the target unit (service), and `-f` (follow) continuously prints new log entries as they are appended to the system journal.
spanish: Para seguir/monitorear los registros de syslog en tiempo real usando journalctl para una unidad systemd específica, utilizas el comando journalctl con las opciones -u <unit> -f.

text: To test if a file exists and is a regular file within a bash conditional statement, you use the operator {{c1::`-f`}}.
explanation: In Bash tests (e.g. `[ -f "$FILE" ]`), `-f` returns true if the path exists and is a regular file, whereas `-d` tests for directories and `-e` checks for existence only.
spanish: Para verificar si un archivo existe y es un archivo regular dentro de una declaración condicional de bash, utilizas el operador -f.

text: To inspect virtual memory statistics, showing processes, memory, paging, block I/O, traps, and CPU activity in real-time, you run the CLI tool {{c1::`vmstat`}}.
explanation: The `vmstat` command reports virtual memory statistics, which helps identify if the system is bottlenecked by swapping (si/so columns) rather than physical memory allocation.
usage: Command: vmstat 2 5 (reports statistics every 2 seconds for 5 iterations)
spanish: Para inspeccionar las estadísticas de la memoria virtual, mostrando procesos, memoria, paginación, E/S de bloques, trampas y actividad de la CPU en tiempo real, ejecutas la herramienta CLI vmstat.

text: To execute a shell command on every file matched by the `find` tool, you append the `-exec` action terminated with the suffix {{c1::`{} \;`}}.
explanation: The `{}` is replaced by the current file path, and `\;` terminates the command executed by `-exec`. Each match spawns a new process of the specified command.
spanish: Para ejecutar un comando en cada archivo encontrado por la herramienta find, utilizas la acción -exec terminada con el sufijo {} \;.
